mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession risk, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when both series grow, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects resilient manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction or becomes statistically insignificant (Rank IC < 0.02) when tested exclusively within late-cycle expansion periods, or 20-day returns are negative despite rising INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance that increases borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for macro surprises in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns when both conditions hold, or the interaction effect shows negative predictive power (Rank IC < -0.02) in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1800 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential construction demand, which drags down related sector earnings and broader economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances where HOUST drops below 1800, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.01) during housing correction regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rising INDPRO, or the signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested macro regimes in a walk-forward evaluation.
